mechanism_narrative: A stable or falling FEDFUNDS rate keeps discount rates from rising, so equity valuations re-rate higher and S&P 500 returns over the next month are positive when policy is not tightening.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher than unconditional returns when FEDFUNDS is flat or declining, or if the sign of the average conditional return is negative in a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: An inflation jump above the prior three-month trend forces tighter policy expectations and lowers equity multiples, so S&P 500 forward returns are negative over the next month after CPIAUCSL acceleration.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above its prior 12-month median
failure_condition: Reject if triggered CPI inflation accelerations are followed by nonnegative SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average 20-day return after triggers exceeds the unconditional mean across a 2-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE signals weakening household income and demand, so equity earnings expectations deteriorate and S&P 500 returns are negative over the next quarter after unemployment is rising.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if rising-unemployment episodes do not produce below-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if PAYEMS growth stays positive while SPX still falls in a way that breaks the labor-demand linkage.

mechanism_narrative: A broad decline in RSAFS indicates weakening nominal demand and lower revenue growth for listed firms, so S&P 500 returns are negative over the next month when retail sales momentum turns down.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month moving average falls below its prior 6-month trend
failure_condition: Reject if downward RSAFS momentum is not followed by below-average 20-day SPX returns, or if the conditional hit rate is not worse than 50% over at least two distinct slowdown episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in INDPRO with concurrent weak HOUST indicates a broad real-activity slowdown, so cyclical earnings expectations fall and S&P 500 returns are negative over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous weakness in INDPRO and HOUST does not forecast lower 60-day SPX returns relative to periods when both are stable or rising, or if the effect disappears after controlling for market trend.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth turns positive after a prior contraction, labor income stabilizes and consumer demand improves, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next week as investors anticipate a recovery.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change turns positive after at least one negative month
failure_condition: Reject if PAYEMS turnarounds are not followed by positive 5-day SPX returns more often than chance, or if the signal is absent when UNRATE is flat or falling.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in HOUST captures housing demand resilience and downstream activity, so S&P 500 returns are positive over the next month after housing starts exceed their recent average.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises more than 5% month over month
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST surges are followed by negative or flat 20-day SPX returns more often than positive ones, or if the average conditional return is not above the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle tightening environment, falling INDPRO together with rising FEDFUNDS signals policy pressure on real activity, which compresses earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with declining INDPRO and rising FEDFUNDS do not produce below-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the conditional effect vanishes outside tightening episodes but is still claimed to be active.
